claim.observation: Volatility3 windows.psscan output lists a process named subject_srv.ex with PID 12528 and PPID 740, Wow64 = True, CreateTime 2018-09-06 19:37:05.000000 UTC and ExitTime N/A. The same psscan output lists PID 740 as services.exe.
claim.interpretation: subject_srv.ex (PID 12528) was launched by parent PID 740, which the same output identifies as services.exe (the Windows Service Control Manager) — i.e. subject_srv.ex is running as a Windows service. It is a 32-bit process (Wow64 = True) with a non-standard name not matching any known Windows/OEM binary, created 2018-09-06 19:37:05 UTC and still running at acquisition (ExitTime N/A). A newly created, oddly-named service binary is a common persistence mechanism. This is the same PID 12528 that owns the TCP 3262 listener verified in F-analyst-SRL18-WKSTN-MEM-001.
